Ayah

قَدْ سَمِعَ ٱللَّهُ قَوْلَ ٱلَّتِى تُجَـٰدِلُكَ فِى زَوْجِهَا وَتَشْتَكِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَٱللَّهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحَاوُرَكُمَآ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (۱)
Allah has certainly heard the speech of her who pleads with you about her husband and complains to Allah. Allah hears the conversation between the two of you. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (1)

Key Points

During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), there was a type of divorce based on which the man no longer had the right to return to his wife, nor did the woman have the right to choose another husband. This type of divorce was called Zihar. During the time of the Prophet of Islam, a man divorced his wife in this manner and addressed her saying: «انتِ علىّ كظهر امّى» (You are to me like the back of my mother). That woman came to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله and explained the situation. The Prophet said: No law has been revealed by Allah concerning this matter. The woman became distressed and said: «أشكو الى اللّه فاقتى و حاجتى و شدة حالى» (I complain to Allah of my destitution, my need, and the severity of my condition). My youth and health are gone. I have children who will be ruined if I entrust them to my husband, and if they stay with me, they will go hungry. It was not long before the verses of this Surah were revealed, commanding that whoever does this must pay an expiation (Kaffarah), and no divorce has actually taken place. Since the husband did not have the means to buy and free a slave, nor was he able to feed sixty needy people, and fasting for two months would also harm him, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله helped him with an appropriate amount. Through the Prophet's assistance, the man fed sixty poor people and returned to his life with this expiation.(1) In this single verse, Allah has spoken about His attribute of being All-Hearing (Sami') three times so that people may converse with Him. «سمع، یسمع، سمیع»

(1) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- We must be careful in our speech, for Allah hears it. «قد سمع اللّه قول الّتى»

2- Allah listens attentively to our supplications and lamentations. «قد سمع اللّه قول الّتى»

3- The noble Prophet, despite his position as the leader of the community, was easily accessible to the public, and people discussed their problems with him. «تجادلك»

4- Freedom of expression and opinion in the Islamic government is such that a woman can argue/dispute with the leader of the community. «الّتى تجادلك»

5- In dead ends (impasses), seek refuge in Allah and His Awliya (saints/representatives). «تشتكى الى اللّه»

6- Events, needs, lamentations, and impasses are the basis for establishing laws. «واللّه یسمع تحاوركما... سمیع بصیر»

7- Being All-Hearing and All-Seeing, and being aware of people's problems, is a condition for the competence of lawmakers in society. «واللّه یسمع تحاوركما... ان اللّه سمیع بصیر»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Mujadalah This Surah has twenty-two verses and was revealed in Medina. It is the only Surah in the Qur'an in which the majestic word "Allah" appears in every single verse. Due to the issue of disputation (Jidal) mentioned in the first verse, this Surah is named Al-Mujadalah (The Disputing Woman). The topics discussed in this Surah are: 1. The law of Zihar, which was a type of divorce during the Age of Ignorance. 2. The positive and negative instances of secret counsel (Najwa). 3. Respect for those who enter an assembly. 4. Distinguishing the Party of Allah (Hizbullah) from the Party of Satan (Hizb ash-Shaytan).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُظَـٰهِرُونَ مِنكُم مِّن نِّسَآئِهِم مَّا هُنَّ أُمَّهَـٰتِهِمْ ۖ إِنْ أُمَّهَـٰتُهُمْ إِلَّا ٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى وَلَدْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَقُولُونَ مُنكَرًا مِّنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ وَزُورًا ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ (۲)
As for those of you who repudiate their wives by zihar, they are not their mothers; their mothers are only those who bore them, and indeed they utter an outrage and a lie. Indeed Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving. (2)

Key Points

In explanation of the previous Ayah, it was stated that during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), Zihar (injurious assimilation) was a type of separation and divorce that occurred by uttering a phrase to this effect: "Intimacy with you is like intimacy with my mother." Islam prohibited this type of divorce and obligated the man, if he performed it, to pay an expiation (Kaffarah). The word Zoor (زور) means false and lying speech. Those who had performed Zihar before the revelation of this Ayah were granted divine forgiveness. "انّ اللّه لعفوّ غفور"

Messages

1- One of the missions of the prophets is to remove superstitions and false traditions among the people. "الّذین یظاهرون..."

2- One of the injustices perpetrated against women during the Age of Ignorance was the law of Zihar, which Islam abolished and for the commission of which it set a heavy penalty. "یظاهرون منكم من نسائكم... یقولون منكراً من القول"

3- The fight against superstitions, habits, and false traditions must be logical and well-reasoned. "ما هنّ أمّهاتهم ان أمّهاتهم الاّ اللاّئى ولدنهم"

4- Sometimes a rightful lamentation leads to a salvific movement. "تشتكى الى اللّه... ان امّهاتهم الاّ اللاّئى ولدنهم"

5- Vile speech and coercion were the continuous practice of the Age of Ignorance. "لیقولون منكرا..."

6- For every action, there is a lawful and legal way. The way for a man and woman to separate is divorce, provided the laws and rights of both are observed. However, separation through Zihar or any other unlawful means is reprehensible (Munkar) and false. "لیقولون منكراً من القول و زورا"

7- Allah both forgives sins and overlooks them. "لعفوّ غفور"

8- No matter how great the sin, divine forgiveness is not to be doubted. "لیقولون منكراً من القول و زورا... لعفو غفور"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُظَـٰهِرُونَ مِن نِّسَآئِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا قَالُوا۟ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَآسَّا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِۦ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۳)
Those who repudiate their wives by ³ih¡r and then retract what they have said, shall set free a slave before they may touch each other. This you are advised [to carry out], and Allah is well aware of what you do. (3)

Key Points

In the Qur'an, freeing a slave is considered an expiation (kaffarah) for accidental killing (2), false oaths (3), and Zihar.

A look at Admonition (Maw'izah) Maw'izah (admonition) means warning and reminding, and it is a blessing intended to soften the heart of the audience and encourage acceptance. It holds a special place in Islam because: Allah admonishes: «انّ اللّه نعمّا یعظكم به» (4) The Prophet is commanded to admonish: «و عِظهم... قولا بلیغا» (5) The Qur'an is an admonition: «هدى و موعظة للمتقین» (6)

Admonition has types: A) Admonition through speech, such as the admonitions of Luqman to his son. «قال لقمان لابنه و هو یعظه» (7) B) Admonition through behavior, which is recommended in narrations: invite people to good deeds not only with your speech but also with your actions. «كونوا دعاة الناس بغیر السنتكم» (8) C) Admonition through punishment and penalty. Just as this verse has made the freeing of a slave a means of taking heed. «ذلكم توعظون به»

In family matters, the Qur'an pays special attention to admonition: A) It tells men: admonish them: «فعظوهنّ» (9) B) Regarding divorce, it states: after the completion of the waiting period (iddah), retention or separation must be accompanied by justice and goodness. «ذلكم یوعظ به» (10) C) In the matter of Zihar, it also admonishes men. «ذلكم توعظون به» D) The interaction of parents with children must be accompanied by admonition. «قال لقمان لابنه و هو یعظه»

2) Nisa, 92. 3) Ma'idah, 89. 4) Nisa, 58. 5) Nisa, 63. 6) Aal-i Imran, 138. 7) Luqman, 13. 8) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 309. 9) Nisa, 34. 10) Talaq, 3.

Messages

1- During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), men oppressed women. «یظاهرون من نسائهم»

2- Islam has no dead ends, and everyone can return to the right path. «یعودون»

3- Penalty plays a preventive role and is a way to control crime. «یظاهرون... فتحریر رقبة»

4- Not every word or speech can be said to one's spouse. Some words carry a heavy expiation. «لما قالوا فتحریر رقبة» We are also responsible for our speech.

5- Oppression of one's spouse has a price (consequence). «فتحریر رقبة»

6- Islam utilizes every opportunity for the freedom of slaves. «فتحریر رقبة»

7- Islam has established penalties to preserve the family structure. «فتحریر رقبة»

8- Restraining people from evil deeds is not always done through speeches; sometimes admonition must take place through penalty. «فتحریر رقبة... توعظون»

9- Islamic penalties are not retaliatory; they are educational. «توعظون به»

10- In paying penalties, there should be no deceit or evasion, for Allah is the Observer. «واللّه بما تعملون خبیر»

Ayah

فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَآسَّا ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَإِطْعَامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِينًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۚ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَلِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٤)
He who cannot afford [to free a slave] shall fast for two successive months before they may touch each other. If he cannot [do so], he shall feed sixty needy persons. This, that you may have faith in Allah and His Apostle. These are Allah’s bounds, and there is a painful punishment for the faithless. (4)

Key Points

The word "حدود" (limits/bounds) has been used more than ten times in the Quran, most of which relate to family rights. Whoever does not find a slave to free must fast for two consecutive months. Based on narrations (Riwāyāt), if someone fasts for 31 consecutive days, they may take the remaining fasts intermittently. And this itself is a facilitation that has been transmitted through the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet). (11) The repetition of the phrase "قبل ان یتماسا" (before they touch each other) in these two verses indicates that contact with the spouse is forbidden before the penalty is paid. (12)

11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Namūneh. 12) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- A universal religion must have laws applicable to every era so that people do not reach a deadlock. Sometimes there is no slave [to free], "فمن لم یجد" (and whoever does not find [one]), and sometimes fasting is not possible. "فمن لم یستطع" (and whoever is not able [to do so]).

2- Islamic penalties encompass various blessings for society: It frees slaves. "فتحریر رقبة" (then [he must] free a slave). It makes individuals patient and resilient through fasting. "فصیام شهرین متتابعین" (then [he must] fast for two consecutive months). It feeds the hungry through feeding [the poor]. "فاطعام ستین مسكینا" (or feed sixty needy people).

3- The penalty must be diverse and graded, taking into account temporal and spatial conditions and the ability of individuals. "فتحریر رقبة... فمن لم یجد... فمن لم یستطع"

4- Divine rulings are subject to reduction (leniency) but are not subject to cancellation (suspension). "فمن لم یجد... فمن لم یستطع..."

5- In Divine punishments and penalties, your wealth or physical ability is reduced, but in return, your faith is increased. "فتحریر رقبة... صیام شهرین... اطعام ستین... ذلك لتؤمنوا"

6- Clarify the philosophy behind penalties and punishments so that people accept them wholeheartedly. "فتحریر رقبة... ذلك لتؤمنوا"

7- Failure to act upon the limits of Allah (حدود اللّه) and disregard for them constitutes disbelief (Kufr). "تلك حدود اللّه و للكافرین"

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحَآدُّونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ كُبِتُوا۟ كَمَا كُبِتَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ وَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۚ وَلِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (٥)
Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle will be subdued just as those who passed before them were subdued. We have certainly sent down manifest signs, and there is a humiliating punishment for the faithless. (5)

Key Points

"یحادّون اللّه" (Yuhāddūna Allāh) means creating obstacles and opposition to the divine limits (Hudūd), whether through hadīd (iron) and power, or through stubbornness, malice, obstruction, and conspiracy. "كُبتوا" (Kubitū) is derived from kabt, meaning to suppress and destroy. The phrase "انّ الّذین یحادّون اللّه و رسوله" (Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger) is repeated twice in this Surah, and in both verses, humiliation and disgrace are mentioned as the punishment for creating obstacles in the path of implementing the divine limits. Just as He says in Ayah 20: "أولئك فى الاذلّین" (Those are among the most humiliated).

Messages

1- Insistence on ignorant customs (Jahiliyyah) and disregard for divine limits constitute war against Allah and the Messenger. "الّذین یظاهرون... فتحریر رقبة... تلك حدود اللّه... الّذین یحادّون اللّه..."

2- In the conflict between truth and falsehood, defeat and disgrace belong to falsehood. "یحادّون اللّه... كبتوا"

3- The significance or warning that Allah has established for faith in Him or war against Him, He has likewise established for faith in His Messenger or war against His Messenger. "لتؤمنوا باللّه و رسوله... یحادّون اللّه و رسوله"

4- Do not create obstacles in the path of implementing divine laws, as this leads to a bitter consequence. "یحادّون اللّه... كبتوا"

5- We must warn the opponents. "یحادّون اللّه... كبتوا"

6- If you doubt the disgrace of the people of falsehood, refer to history. "كما كبت الّذین من قبلهم"

7- History and historical events follow a law and a tradition (Sunnah). "كما كبتوا كبت..."

8- The truth has always had enemies. "كما كبت الّذین من قبلهم"

9- Divine wrath follows the completion of the argument (proof). "كبت الّذین من قبلهم و قد أنزلنا آیات..."

10- The signs of Allah are both clear and numerous. "آیات بیّنات"

11- Contention against the Prophets is an insult to them, and thus the punishment for such individuals is humiliation and disgrace. "وللكافرین عذاب مهین"

12- Creating obstacles and contending against the truth is either disbelief (Kufr) itself or leads to disbelief. "وللكافرین عذاب مهین"

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوٓا۟ ۚ أَحْصَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ وَنَسُوهُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (٦)
The day when Allah will raise them all together, He will inform them about what they have done. Allah has kept account of it, while they have forgotten, and Allah is witness to all things. (6)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the resurrection of all people on the Day of Judgment, collectively, the example of which we read elsewhere: «حشرناهم فلم نغادر منهم احدا»(13) (We shall raise everyone in the ranks of the gathering and leave out no one.) And the example of Allah's enumeration we read in another Ayah: «...ما لهذا الكتاب لایغادر صغیرة و لاكبیرة الاّ أحصاها...»(14) (What kind of book is this that has not left out our small and great deeds, but has counted them all.) The word «شهید» (Shahīd) in the Quran sometimes means the witnessed (مشهود) and sometimes means the witness (شاهد). Here, based on the context of «أحصاه اللّه...», it means the witness. Of course, alongside the testimony and witnessing of Allah, there are other witnesses as well. «یوم یقوم الاشهاد»(15)

13) Kahf, 47. 14) Kahf, 49. 15) Ghafir, 51.

Messages

1- Divine wrath exists both in this world, «كبتوا» (they were disgraced), and in the Hereafter. «یوم یبعثهم اللّه» (The Day when Allah resurrects them).

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the scene where Divine power and knowledge are manifested. «یبعثهم اللّه جمیعا» (Allah resurrects them all) is a sign of His power, and «فینبّئهم» (then He will inform them) is a sign of His knowledge.

3- The court of the Day of Judgment is public and universal. «جمیعاً فینبئهم»

4- The transgressor must be made to understand his wrongdoings. «فینبئهم بما عملوا»

5- The contentious ones should know that their plots and obstructions are recorded and registered, even if they themselves forget and lose memory of them. «احصاه اللّه... نسوه»

6- Do not overlook your small transgressions, for Allah keeps an accurate account of everything. «أحصاه اللّه»

7- Forgetting transgressions does not cause them to be erased. «أحصاه اللّه و نسوه»

8- The world is the presence of Allah; let us not commit sin in the presence of Allah. «واللّه على كلّ شى ء شهید»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ مَا يَكُونُ مِن نَّجْوَىٰ ثَلَـٰثَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ رَابِعُهُمْ وَلَا خَمْسَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ سَادِسُهُمْ وَلَآ أَدْنَىٰ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْثَرَ إِلَّا هُوَ مَعَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كَانُوا۟ ۖ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۷)
Have you not regarded that Allah knows whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth? There dost not takes place any secret talk among three, but He is their fourth [companion], nor among five but He is their sixth, nor when they are less than that or more but He is with them wherever they may be. Then He will inform them about what they have done on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. (7)

Key Points

Tar (تَر) derives from ru'yah (رؤیت), meaning seeing, which is sometimes used to mean understanding, and this latter meaning is intended here. Najwa (نجوى) derives from najwah (نجوَة), meaning a high place separated from other parts of the earth. Since whispering (secret conversation) is essentially separating the party from others, or because it is spoken so softly that others are unaware of it, or because the content of the speech serves as a means for the listener to avoid danger, it is called najwa. Najwa also includes secret meetings held by criminals and transgressors away from the eyes of others, where they speak softly and without the slightest noise, assuming that no one sees them or hears their speech.

The Quran has mentioned Allah's knowledge of people's secrets and najwa several times, including: Ayah 78 of Surah At-Tawbah: «ألم یعلموا انّ اللّه یعلم سرّهم و نجواهم و انّ اللّه علاّم الغیوب» and Ayah 80 of Surah Az-Zukhruf: «ام یحسبون انّا لانسمع سرّهم و نجواهم بلى و رسلنا لدیهم یكتبون»

In this Ayah and the previous Ayah, Allah's knowledge has been mentioned ten times, and the beginning and end of this Ayah are marked by Divine knowledge. «یعلم... علیم»

By looking at the preceding verses, we realize the characteristics of Divine knowledge: His knowledge encompasses all details. «هو رابعهم، هو سادسهم» His knowledge is precise. «أحصاه اللّه» His knowledge is immediate (present). «على كلّ شى ء شهید» The dust of forgetfulness does not settle upon His knowledge. «احصاه اللّه و نسوه» Through His knowledge, others become aware of their forgotten deeds. «نسوه... ینبئهم» Allah's comprehensive encompassment of all existence is eternal and everlasting. «هو معهم این ما كانوا» He knows, but usually He does not expose anyone in this world. «ینبّئهم بما عملوا یوم القیامة» His knowledge is permanent, not seasonal. «یعلم، علیم» His knowledge of the heavens and the earth is uniform. «یعلم ما فى السّموات و ما فى الارض»

Messages

1- A gaze that does not lead to knowing and understanding the truth is subject to reproach. «ألم تر...»

2- The heavens and the earth, the great and the small, the manifest and the hidden, are all equal before Allah's knowledge. «یعلم ما فى السّموات و ما فى الارض... هو رابعهم... هو معهم»

3- The multiplicity of individuals and the diversity of their speech do not affect Allah's knowledge, and He is aware of all of them and their words. «لا ادنى من ذلك و لااكثر»

4- Since Allah's existence is limitless, He is with us wherever we may be. «هو معهم أینما كانوا»

5- Place and time have no effect on Allah's knowledge, and He is aware of everything. «هو معهم أینما كانوا»

6- On the Day of Resurrection, the Informer Himself was the Witness. «شهید، علیم، ینبئهم»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ نُهُوا۟ عَنِ ٱلنَّجْوَىٰ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا نُهُوا۟ عَنْهُ وَيَتَنَـٰجَوْنَ بِٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَٰنِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ ٱلرَّسُولِ وَإِذَا جَآءُوكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ ٱللَّهُ وَيَقُولُونَ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَوْلَا يُعَذِّبُنَا ٱللَّهُ بِمَا نَقُولُ ۚ حَسْبُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ يَصْلَوْنَهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۸)
Have you not regarded those who were forbidden from secret talks but again resumed what they had been forbidden from, and hold secret talks [imbued] with sin and transgression and disobedience to the Apostle? And when they come to you they greet you with words with which Allah never greeted you, and they say to themselves, ‘Why does not Allah punish us for what we say?!’ Let hell suffice them: they shall enter it, and it is an evil destination! (8)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that no matter how many people whisper together, Allah is present with them and hears their speech. This Ayah presents an example of whispering (najwa), which is the whispering of opponents regarding their opposition to the Prophet. "یصلونها" (yaslawna-hā) is derived from ṣalā, meaning to enter a place for the purpose of burning and melting (16). Man gradually commits greater sins: Ithm (sin) is an individual sin, such as drinking wine. ʿUdwān (transgression) is violating the rights of others. Maʿṣiyat al-Rasūl (disobedience to the Messenger) is opposing the social and governmental commands issued by the divine leader for the welfare of the Muslims (17).

16) Tafsir al-Mizan. 17) Tafsir al-Mizan and Namūneh.

Messages

1- Whispering (najwa) among the community of believers is an evil (munkar) that has been forbidden. "نُهوا عن النجوى" (They were forbidden from whispering) (In Ayah 10, we also read: "انّما النّجوى من الشیطان" - Indeed, secret counsel is only from Satan).

2- Those who enjoin good and forbid evil should not expect people to abandon the evil merely because they have been told to do so. "یعودون لما نهوا" (They return to that which they were forbidden).

3- Repeating sin and ignoring warnings makes the crime heavier. "یعودون لما نهوا" (They return to that which they were forbidden).

4- Obedience to the infallible Prophet is obligatory. (Therefore, disobedience to him in any form is criticized.) "معصیة الرسول" (Disobedience to the Messenger).

5- We should not trust every form of glorification, honor, or praise. (The hypocrites openly offer excessive glorification, but secretly speak of disobeying the Prophet.) "حیّوك بما لم یحیّك به اللّه" (They greet you with that which Allah has not greeted you).

6- Flattery, sycophancy, and excessive praise are signs of hypocrisy. "حیّوك بما لم یحیّك به اللّه" (They greet you with that which Allah has not greeted you).

7- Even when glorifying the noblest of creation, we must observe the limits. "لم یحیك به اللّه" (Allah has not greeted you).

8- Allah not only reveals the secret speech of the hypocrites but also exposes what they hold in their hearts, which is, in effect, self-talk (hadith al-nafs). "یقولون فى أنفسهم" (They say within themselves).

9- Do not consider divine respites as a sign of His satisfaction. "لولا یعذّبنا اللّه حسبهم جهنم" (Why does Allah not punish us? Sufficient for them is Hell).

10- The ultimate end of hypocrisy is Hell. "حسبهم جهنّم" (Sufficient for them is Hell).

11- Evil speech prepares a terrible end for man. "بما نقول... فبئس المصیر" (for what we say... then wretched is the destination).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا تَنَـٰجَيْتُمْ فَلَا تَتَنَـٰجَوْا۟ بِٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَٰنِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ ٱلرَّسُولِ وَتَنَـٰجَوْا۟ بِٱلْبِرِّ وَٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِىٓ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۹)
O you who have faith! When you converse secretly, do not hold private conversations [imbued] with sin and aggression and disobedience to the Apostle, but converse in [a spirit of] piety and Godfearing, and be wary of Allah toward whom you will be gathered. (9)

Key Points

«بِرّ» (righteousness/goodness) includes every good deed, and examples of it are mentioned in Ayah 177 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «ولكنّ البرّ من آمن باللّه...» Whispering (Najwa) and speaking privately can have different rulings, including: Where the disclosure of secrets would harm Islam and Muslims, whispering is obligatory (wajib). Where it causes fear and anxiety in others, whispering is forbidden (haram). And where it is for helping others and recommending piety (taqwa), it is recommended (mustahabb). The subject and content of the speech determine its value. If the subject of the whispering is goodness and piety, it is a virtue, but if it is sin, oppression, and conspiracy, it is forbidden (haram). Of course, sometimes the content is good, but the situation is not appropriate. For example, two people whispering in a gathering of believers.

Messages

1- The believer is respected; preserve his respect even when performing Nahy 'anil Munkar (forbidding evil). «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا»

2- In Amr bil Ma'ruf (enjoining good), Nahy 'anil Munkar (forbidding evil), guidance, and propagation, the paths of truth and falsehood and their manifestations must be clarified. «لاتتناجوا... تناجوا»

3- Not all speech should be spoken openly or secretly. Sometimes Nahy 'anil Munkar or Amr bil Ma'ruf must be done in secret and privately. «تناجوا بالبرّ و التقوى»

4- Beware lest we mistake bad whispering for good whispering. «تناجوا... واتقوا اللّه الّذى الیه تحشرون»

5- Piety (taqwa), abstinence, and attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are the basis for avoiding improper speech. «واتّقوا اللّه الّذى الیه تحشرون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلنَّجْوَىٰ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ لِيَحْزُنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَلَيْسَ بِضَآرِّهِمْ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٠)
Indeed [malicious] secret talks are from Satan, that he may upset the faithful, but he cannot harm them in any way except by Allah’s leave, and in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (10)

Messages

1- Just as extravagance and excessive consumption make a person a brother of Satan, «انّ المبذّرین كانوا اخوان الشّیاطین»(18), whispering (or secret counsel) also makes a person Satan-like. «انّما النّجوى من الشّیطان»

2- Any kind of speech, whether open or secret, that causes fear and anxiety among Muslims, is a Satanic act. «النّجوى من الشّیطان لیحزن الّذین آمنوا»

3- Worries must be consoled in this way: that unless Allah wills it, no one can inflict harm. «لیحزن... لیس بضارّهم»

4- Believers are under the protection and guardianship of Allah. «و لیس بضارهم شیئاً»

5- The efforts of the hypocrites are not outside the sphere of Divine power. «و لیس بضارهم شیئاً الا باذن اللّه»

6- We must neutralize the psychological warfare of the enemy. «لیحزن... و لیس بضارهم شیئاً»

7- The healing remedy for any kind of unrest and anxiety is reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah. «و على اللّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

8- Reliance upon Allah is not for everyone; it requires faith. «فلیتوكّل المؤمنون» Although Allah protects the believers, they must also rely upon Him. (18) Al-Isra, 27.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا۟ فِى ٱلْمَجَـٰلِسِ فَٱفْسَحُوا۟ يَفْسَحِ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِذَا قِيلَ ٱنشُزُوا۟ فَٱنشُزُوا۟ يَرْفَعِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ دَرَجَـٰتٍ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۱۱)
O you who have faith! When you are told, ‘Make room,’ in sittings, then do make room; Allah will make room for you. And when you are told, ‘Rise up!’ Do rise up. Allah will raise in rank those of you who have faith and those who have been given knowledge, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (11)

Key Points

Tafassahu (تفسّح) means to expand, make room, and accommodate, while Inshizu (انشزوا) means to rise from one's place out of respect for others. In the previous Ayah, He mentioned secret counsel (najwa) as the cause of sorrow and distress; in this Ayah, He mentions making room for others to sit in gatherings as the cause of joy and affection. (19)

Allah has designated special ranks and stations in the Quran for several groups: the Prophets, the strivers (mujahidin), those who perform prayer, those who give charity (infaq), the believers who perform righteous deeds, and the scholars. Perhaps the phrase «یرفع اللّه الّذین آمنوا منكم و الّذین أوتوا العلم درجات» (Allah will raise those among you who believe and those who have been given knowledge by [many] degrees) indicates that the command to rise is due to the entry of believers and scholars, meaning, stand up out of respect for them. (20)

An anecdote: The late Tabarsi narrates in the book Al-Ihtijaj that during the time of Imam Hadi عليه السلام, one of the Shia jurists (fuqaha), who had enlightened a misguided Nasibi (enemy of Ahl al-Bayt) in a debate and proven his own righteousness, entered a gathering where Alawites and Banu Hashim were also present. Imam Hadi عليه السلام seated that jurist in the best place in the gathering. This great respect was difficult for the Alawites and Banu Hashim to accept. Their leader opened his mouth in protest, asking why the Imam had preferred such a person over them. The Imam said: Are you satisfied with the judgment of the Quran? They said: Yes. The حضرت then recited this Ayah: «تفسحوا فى المجالس... و اذا قیل لكم انشزوا فانشزوا یرفع اللّه الّذین آمنوا منكم و الّذین اوتوا العلم درجات» The worth of this person, due to the scholarly debate and the defeat he inflicted upon that Nasibi, is greater than any honor or lineage. (21)

Yes, the believing scholar is superior to the ordinary believer, just as the believer is superior to the non-believer. The Quran states elsewhere: «هل یستوى الّذین یعلمون والّذین لایعلمون» (Are those who know equal to those who do not know?) (22)

(19) Tafsir Maraghi. (20) Tafsir Namuneh. (21) Tafsir Furqan. (22) Zumar, 9.

Messages

1- Faith has conditions and requirements that must be observed. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اذا...»

2- Making room and showing respect to newcomers is a virtue. «فافسحوا... فانشزوا»

3- Making room for others is necessary regardless of the type of gathering. «فى المجالس»

4- Acting upon Allah’s commands is the basis for receiving greater rewards. «فافسحوا یفسح اللّه لكم»

5- Facilitating ease for others causes Allah to facilitate ease in your affairs. «فافسحوا یفسح اللّه لكم» The Divine reward is commensurate with the action. Ease brings ease.

6- Observing social etiquette, even in sitting down and standing up, is recommended by Islam. «فافسحوا... فانشزوا»

7- Sometimes it is necessary to tell the attendees of a gathering to rise out of respect for the entry of dignitaries and scholars. «انشزوا فانشزوا»

8- Knowledge is a gift and a favor from Allah. «اوتوا العلم...»

9- The possessors of knowledge and learning must enjoy a high and elevated status in the Islamic society. «و الّذین اوتوا العلم درجات»

10- The action of a scholar carries a greater reward. (23) «یرفع اللّه... الّذین اوتوا العلم درجات»

11- Making room and rising should be sincere and for the sake of Allah, not for other motives. «واللّه بما تعملون خبیر»

12- In sitting down and standing up, let us not forget Allah. «واللّه بما تعملون خبیر»

(23) Tafsir Tibyan.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نَـٰجَيْتُمُ ٱلرَّسُولَ فَقَدِّمُوا۟ بَيْنَ يَدَىْ نَجْوَىٰكُمْ صَدَقَةً ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ تَجِدُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۲)
O you who have faith! When you converse privately with the Apostle, offer a charity before your private talk. That is better for you and purer. But if you cannot afford [to make the offering], then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (12)

Key Points

A group of people, especially the wealthy, constantly bothered the Prophet and spoke with him privately, an act that caused distress to others or secured undue privilege and closeness for themselves. The Ayah was revealed to serve as a test of their true devotion. Of course, this ruling was temporary and experimental, and the command was lifted after a period of time.(24) Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The Ayah that no one acted upon before me or after me is the Ayah of charity (sadaqah). I had one dinar, which I converted into ten dirhams. Every time I wanted to have a private conversation (najwa) with the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, I would give one dirham in charity.(25) Purity (taharah) is of two types: 1. Physical and apparent, «و ان كنتم جنباً فاطّهروا»(26) 2. Spiritual and inner. «ذلك خیر لكم و اطهر»

24) Tafsir Namuneh. 25) Tafasir Kanz al-Daqa'iq, Namuneh, and Durr al-Manthur. 26) Al-Ma'idah, 6.

Messages

1- Faith brings responsibility. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اذا...»

2- Charity (sadaqah) must be paid before performing the action. «فقدّموا... صدقة»

3- A teacher may specify a cost for instruction, whether He receives it Himself or directs it to others. «فقدّموا بین یدى نجواكم صدقة»

4- The amount of charity was not specified so that everyone could decide for themselves. «صدقة»

5- Every means must be used for poverty alleviation. The condition for a private meeting with the Prophet was paying charity to the poor. «فقدّموا بین یدى نجواكم صدقة»

6- When choosing between money and the Prophet, the level of people's faith and devotion is revealed. «فقدّموا بین یدى نجواكم صدقة»

7- Charity, social laws, and restrictions bring many benefits (khayrat) for the individual and society. «ذلك خیر»

8- Attachment to the world is impurity, and detachment from it is purity. «ذلك خیر لكم و اطهر»

9- Charity is a type of purification (tat'hir). «اطهر» As we read elsewhere: «خذ من أموالهم صدقة تطهرّهم»(27)

10- There are no dead ends in Islamic rulings. «فان لم تجدوا...»

11- The Lawgiver must consider all people when establishing a law. «فان لم تجدوا...»

12- The poor should not feel inferior due to their inability to give charity, as they are covered by Divine Mercy. «فان لم تجدوا فان اللّه غفور رحیم»

13- Islam has never rejected or deprived anyone because of poverty. «فان لم تجدوا فانّ اللّه غفور رحیم»27) At-Tawbah, 103.

Ayah

ءَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَن تُقَدِّمُوا۟ بَيْنَ يَدَىْ نَجْوَىٰكُمْ صَدَقَـٰتٍ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ وَتَابَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۳)
Were you dismayed by having to offer charities before your private talks? Since you did not do it, and Allah has excused you [for not be able to comply], now maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle. Allah is well aware of what you do. (13)

Key Points

"Ishfāq" (اشفاق) means anxiety and apprehension due to the fear of losing something. (28) This Ayah indicates that the payment of charity (sadaqah) before private conversation with the Prophet was initially a test command, intended to distinguish sincere and devoted believers from others. It was not that Allah retreated [from the command] because He saw that people were not acting upon it. In this Ayah, some companions of the Prophet who abandoned private conversation with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله due to fear of spending wealth, regardless of the amount, are subjected to divine reproach.

28) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Although private consultation (najwā) with the Prophet was neither obligatory nor recommended, they were subjected to reproach because they acted stingily. «ءاشفقتم»

2- Not all companions of the Noble Prophet were obedient to him. «فاذ لم تفعلوا» (They did not act upon the explicit command: «قدّموا بین یدى نجواكم صدقة»).

3- If you do not perform the recommended acts, at least do not fall short in performing the obligatory duties. Allah tells those who were unwilling to pay charity: Pay the Zakat. «ءاشفقتم ان تقدموا... صدقات... اتوا الزكاة»

4- Reproach must be accompanied by a path to compensation (or rectification). «ءاشفقتم... تاب اللّه علیكم فأقیموا الصّلاة...»

5- Do not despair of those who disobey, and deal with them justly. Allah demands obedience from those who failed to obey another command. «لم تفعلوا... أطیعوا اللّه»

6- Attention to prayer (Salat) and Zakat compensates for missed benefits (or neglected duties). «لم تفعلوا... فاقیموا الصّلاة و آتواالزكاة»

7- Religion is not merely an individual and personal relationship with God; caring for the poor and obeying the governmental commands of the Prophet are also among the religious rulings. «فاقیموا الصّلاة و آتوا الزكاة و اطیعوا اللّه و رسوله»

8- We must be precise in how we act, for Allah is aware of all deeds. «واللّه خبیر بما تعملون»

Ayah

۞ أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا۟ قَوْمًا غَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مَّا هُم مِّنكُمْ وَلَا مِنْهُمْ وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى ٱلْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٤)
Have you not regarded those who befriend a people at whom Allah is wrathful? They neither belong to you, nor to them, and they swear false oaths [that they are with you] and they know. (14)

Ayah

أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ إِنَّهُمْ سَآءَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٥)
Allah has prepared a severe punishment for them. Evil indeed is what they used to do. (15)

Key Points

Although various groups have incurred Allah's wrath in the Quran, perhaps the intended meaning of the 'people who incurred wrath' in this and other verses is the Jews, because they are mentioned three times in the Quran with the phrase "باؤوا بغضب" (29). Those who initiated friendship with the group that incurred wrath are the hypocrites.

29) Baqarah 60 and 91, Aal-i-Imran, 112.

Messages

1- Accepting the guardianship and leadership of those who have incurred Allah's wrath is a reprehensible matter. «الم تر الى الّذین...»

2- Whoever removes his hand from the hand of the Prophet of Mercy places it in the hand of those throughout history who have incurred wrath. «تولّوا... غضب اللّه علیهم»

3- Distinguishing insiders from outsiders and recognizing the enemy and the hypocrite is necessary. «ما هم منكم و لامنهم»

4- The habit and method of the hypocrites is resorting to false oaths. «یحلفون على الكذب»

5- Hypocrites constantly misuse sacred things. «یحلفون»

6- Hypocrites knowingly commit wrongdoings. «و هم یعلمون»

7- Divine punishment and retribution are due to the evil actions of human beings. «ساء ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (۱٦)
They make a shield of their oaths and bar [people] from the way of Allah; so there is a humiliating punishment for them. (16)

Ayah

لَّن تُغْنِىَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱۷)
Their possessions and children will not avail them in any way against Allah. They shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]. (17)

Key Points

To lead people astray and deter them from the path of truth, the devils among jinn and mankind employ various methods mentioned in the Quran, including: False oaths, which is the subject of this very Ayah. Material investments. «ینفقون أموالهم لیصدّوا عن سبیل اللّه»(30) Cultural investment and the spread and propagation of corruption. «من النّاس من یشترى لهو الحدیث لیضلّ عن سبیل اللّه»(31) Innovation (Bid'ah) and creating new doctrines. «و جعل للّه أنداداً لیضلّ عن سبیله»(32) Making evils appear beautiful and decorating deeds. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان أعمالهم فصدّهم عن السّبیل»(33)

30) Anfal, 36. 31) Luqman, 6. 32) Zumar, 8. 33) Naml, 24.

Messages

1- The hypocrites constantly and perpetually use religion against religion. «اتخذوا ایمانهم جُنّة»

2- The punishment for the hypocrites is severe. «فلهم عذاب مهین... فیها خالدون»

3- The warnings of the Quran are very serious. Wealth and children will be ineffective in the Hereafter. «لن تغنى عنهم أموالهم و لا أولادهم...»

4- This world has many differences compared to the Hereafter, among which is the ineffectiveness of wealth and children in the Hereafter. «لن تغنى عنهم أموالهم و لا أولادهم...»

5- When giving warnings, address the causes. The cause of the conspiracies is being deceived by wealth and children. «لن تغنى عنهم أموالهم و لا أولادهم...»

6- Hypocrisy in this world leads to eternity in Hellfire. «أصحاب النّار هم فیها خالدون»

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُۥ كَمَا يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱۸)
The day when Allah will raise them all together, they will swear to Him, just like they swear to you [now], supposing that they stand on something. Look! They are indeed liars! (18)

Key Points

The issue of false oaths on the Day of Resurrection has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. Just as we read in Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 23: «واللّه ربّنا ما كنا مشركین» (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists). They swear that they were not polytheists. Since an oath gradually leads a person to belief (or acceptance), one must stand against false oaths and not allow people to believe them. In the phrase «الا انهم هم الكاذبون», the words «الا, انّ, هم» are all intended to prevent people from believing (the hypocrites). Lying is one of the signs of the hypocrite in this world and the Hereafter. In Surah Al-Munafiqun, we read that Allah considers the testimony of the hypocrites regarding the truthfulness of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله to be false. «واللّه یشهد ان المنافقین لكاذبون» And in this Ayah, Allah considers them liars even on the Day of Resurrection.

Messages

1- If lying becomes an ingrained habit (malakah) and a permanent, constant characteristic for a person, he will swear falsely even on the Day of Resurrection. «فیحلفون له كما یحلفون لكم»

2- Hypocrites are immersed in an imaginary world. «یحسبون انهم على شى ء»

3- The lives of the hypocrites are entirely falsehood and untruth. «ألا انّهم هم الكاذبون»

Ayah

ٱسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فَأَنسَىٰهُمْ ذِكْرَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حِزْبُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۱۹)
Satan has prevailed upon them, so he has caused them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are Satan’s confederates. Look! Indeed, it is Satan’s confederates who are the losers! (19)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: If truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil) are presented clearly, no problem arises, but mixing truth and falsehood causes the emergence of discord (Fitnah) and the dominance of Satan (Shaytan). «و لكن یؤخذ من هذا ضِغث و من هذا ضغث فیمزجان فهنالك یستولى الشّیطان على أولیائه»(34) Imam Husayn عليه السلام also said to Yazid's army in Karbala: «استحوذ الشّیطان على أولیائه».(35)

Satan's method is like this: He draws man towards him step by step. «خطوات الشّیطان»(36) Sometimes He casts a suggestion, like someone throwing a stone from afar. «القى الشّیطان...»(37) Sometimes He makes contact but has no penetration. «مسّهم طائف من الشّیطان»(38) Sometimes He penetrates the heart. «یوسوس فى صدور النّاس»(39) Sometimes He penetrates the heart and remains there. «فهو له قرین»(40) Sometimes man becomes the brother of Satan. «اخوان الشّیاطین»(41) And sometimes he becomes a follower of Satan, and Satan gains complete dominance over him. «استحوذ علیهم الشّیطان»

Messages

1- If a believer comes into contact with Satan, he remembers Allah, «مسهم طائف من الشّیطان تذكروا»(42) but the hypocrite, who has moved from the stage of contact to the stage of accepting Satan's dominance, no longer remembers Him. «فانساهم ذكر اللّه»

2- False oaths are the prelude to Satan's dominance. «یحلفون... استحوذ...»

3- The more heedless we become of the remembrance of Allah, the closer we approach the orbit of the party of Satan. «انساهم ذكر اللّه... حزب الشّیطان»

4- We should not place ourselves at the disposal of every party or group, as some groups are the party of Satan. «أولئك حزب الشّیطان»

5- Real loss is following Satan, not financial losses. «انّ حزب الشّیطان هم الخاسرون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحَآدُّونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْأَذَلِّينَ (۲٠)
Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle—they will be among the most abased. (20)

Key Points

Apparently, this Ayah is a continuation of the previous verses and the ongoing actions of the hypocrites, especially considering that in the fifth Ayah of this very Surah, we read about the downfall and destruction of hostile positioning against Allah and the Messenger: «یحادّون اللّه و رسوله كبتوا كما كبت الّذین من قبلهم». The difficulties that believers sometimes endure are transient, momentary, and removable. «و لقد نصركم اللّه ببدر و أنتم اذلّة»(43) But the humiliation of the opponents is eternal, and their honor is temporary. «اولئك فى الاذلّین».

43) Aal-i-Imran, 123.

Messages

1- Hypocrisy and duplicity lead a person to the point where he places himself in opposition to Allah and the Messenger. «یحادّون اللّه و رسوله»

2- Just as the commands of the Prophet are the commands of Allah, war against the Messenger of Allah is war against Allah. «الّذین یحادّون اللّه و رسوله»

3- Whoever stands against the truth will suffer the worst forms of humiliation. «یحادّون اللّه... فى الاذلّین»

4- Those who considered the Muslims humiliated, «لئن رجعنا الى المدینه لیخرجنّ الاعزّ منها الاذلّ»(44), will themselves endure the worst kind of humiliation. «فى الاذلین»

5- Since honor belongs solely to Allah and His Messenger, those who take a stand against Them become the most humiliated and abject individuals. «فى الاذلّین»

6- The ultimate consequence of opposing the commands of Allah and the Prophet is disgrace and humiliation in this world and the Hereafter. «أولئك فى الاذلین» 44) Al-Munafiqun, 8.

Ayah

كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا۠ وَرُسُلِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (۲۱)
Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles.’ Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (21)

Key Points

The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is the victory of truth over falsehood. Despite all the accusations leveled against the Prophet, and all the obstructions, hindrances, instigations of discord (fitnah), and wars, is the name of the Prophet of Islam and the other Prophets more prominent today, or the names of their opponents? Is the name of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) remaining, or Nimrod? Is Imam Husayn عليه السلام cherished, or Yazid? Imam Husayn عليه السلام achieved His goal, which was to expose the Umayyads and teach humanity the lesson of freedom, but what about Yazid?

Question

If truth is victorious, then why do its followers sometimes suffer defeat?

Answer

If it is said that a child is a blessing (ni'mah), this does not mean that the difficulties of pregnancy and childbirth are absent; rather, the intention is that despite all these difficulties, it remains a blessing. The victory of truth over falsehood is likewise achieved under the shadow of faith, steadfastness, and unity, just as the Quran states: «الّذين قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثم استقاموا تتنزّل عليهم الملائكة»(45), «لاتهنوا و لاتحزنوا و أنتم الاعلون ان كنتم مؤمنين»(46). However, if weakness, disunity, and disobedience appear, defeat will be certain: «حتّى اذا فشلتم و تنازعتم فى الامر و عصيتم»(47).

Based on narrations (riwayat), the promise of Allah regarding the complete dominance of truth over falsehood will be realized during the advent (Zuhur) of Imam al-Zaman عليه السلام.

Ayah

لَّا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ يُوَآدُّونَ مَنْ حَآدَّ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَوْ كَانُوٓا۟ ءَابَآءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَٰنَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَتَبَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ ۖ وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ رَضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حِزْبُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۲۲)
You will not find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day endearing those who oppose Allah and His Apostle even though they were their own parents, or children, or brothers, or kinsfolk. [For] such, He has written faith into their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. He will admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. They are Allah’s confederates. Look! The confederates of Allah are indeed felicitous! (22)

Key Points

The station of Riḍā (contentment/satisfaction) is higher than the station of Taslīm (submission). This means that the servant is not only submissive to the Divine decrees and His commands and prohibitions, but is also content with them in his heart and accepts them out of satisfaction.

Faith is incompatible with disbelief. Regarding Noah's son, it is said: «انه لیس من اهلك» (48). When it became clear to Abraham that his uncle was deviant, he disassociated himself from him. «فلما تبیّن له انه عدوّ للّه تبرّأ منه» (49). Even the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is forbidden from seeking forgiveness for the polytheists. «ما كان للنبى والّذین آمنوا ان یستغفروا للمشركین ولو كانوا اولى قربى من بعد ما تبین لهم انهم اصحاب الجحیم» (50).

Tawallī and Tabarrī (Loving and Disassociating): The issue of Tawallī (loving the friends of God) and Tabarrī (disassociating from the enemies of God) has been raised repeatedly in Sūrat al-Mujādalah, indicating the importance of friendship with the friends of Allah and enmity toward the enemies of Allah: «تولّوا قوماً غضب اللّه علیهم», «لاتجد... یوادون من حادّ اللّه».

Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said: «كمال الدین ولایتنا والبرائة من عدّونا» The perfection of religion lies in accepting our Wilāyah (guardianship) and disassociating from our enemies.

It was said to Imam Ṣādiq عليه السلام: So-and-so loves you but acts weakly regarding disassociating from your enemies. The Imam said: He lies, the one who claims to love us yet has not distanced himself from our enemy (51).

Imam Ṣādiq عليه السلام said: «اوحى اللّه الى نبىّ من انبیائه قل للمؤمنین لاتلبسوا لباس اعدایى و لاتطعموا مطاعم اعدایى و لاتسلكوا مسالك اعدایى فتكونوا اعدایى كما هم اعدایى» (52). Allah the Almighty revealed to one of His prophets: Tell the believers: Do not wear the clothes of My enemies, do not eat the food of My enemies, and do not follow the paths of My enemies in lifestyle and behavior, for in that case, you will be counted as My enemies, just as they are My enemies.

It is stated in a ḥadīth: «هل الدین الاّ الحبّ والبغض» (53). Is religion anything but love and hate?

Types of Divine Support in the Quran: Support through His angel Gabriel. «أیّدتك بروح القدس» (54). Special Divine support. «و أیّدهم بروح منه». Support through the help of the believers. «ایّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین» (55). Support through unseen Divine armies. «أیّده بجنود لم تروها» (56).

The Quran uses the phrase «رضى اللّه عنهم و رضوا عنه» in several instances: 1. Regarding the truthful and righteous in action: «یوم ینفع الصّادقین صدقهم... رضى اللّه...» (57). 2. Regarding the pioneers and forerunners: «والسّابقون الاولون... رضى اللّه...» (58). 3. Regarding the true believers: «كتب فى قلوبهم الایمان... رضى اللّه...»

Taking a stand against Allah and His Messenger is so ugly and reprehensible that it is mentioned four times in the Quran, and each time it is accompanied by Divine threat and wrath. This includes verses 5, 20, and 22 of this very Sūrah, which entail various forms of threat, such as: Being overthrown. «كبتوا». Being humiliated. «فى الاذلین». Being subjected to displeasure. «لاتجد قوماً... یوادّون من حاد اللّه و رسوله...». And entering the Fire and eternal dwelling in Hell. «من یحادد اللّه... له نار... خالداً...» (59).

Messages

  1. One heart cannot hold two loves. Friendship with Allah is incompatible with friendship toward the enemies of Allah's religion. «لاتجد قوماً یؤمنون... یوادّون من حادّ اللّه» (In another verse, we have: «ما جعل اللّه لرجل من قلبین فى جوفه» (60). Allah has not placed two hearts inside a man's chest.)

  2. Eliminate faults from the root. Affection for the enemies of Allah compels a person toward their intellectual, practical, and moral inclinations; therefore, its root must be dried up. «لاتجد... یوادّون من حادّ اللّه»

  3. In Islam, relationships and affections must be within the sphere of Allah's satisfaction. «لاتجد قوماً یؤمنون»

  4. Family and kinship relations also have a red line and boundaries. «و لو كانوا آبائهم...»

  5. Because of cutting ties with four groups of relatives if they are hostile (father, son, brother, and kin), Allah has mentioned four examples of His grace: Faith, support, Paradise, and contentment. «الایمان، ایّدهم، جنّات، رضى اللّه»

  6. The value of faith lies in its stability; otherwise, temporary, seasonal, and borrowed faiths (61) are numerous. «كتب فى قلوبهم الایمان»

  7. First, stable faith, then support and reception. «كتب فى قلوبهم الایمان... ایّدهم...»

  8. Religion is not merely a heartfelt and individual relationship between man and Allah, but it also determines social relationships. «لو كانوا آبائهم...»

  9. Rewards are not limited to the Hereafter; rather, the stabilization of faith and Divine support are among the Divine rewards in this world. «كتب فى قلوبهم... ایّدهم»

  10. The Party of Allah (Ḥizb Allāh) does not befriend the enemy of Allah. «لاتجد قوماً... یوادّون من حادّ اللّه... أولئك حزب اللّه»

  11. The Party of Allah has no specific race, language, or region; rather, every resilient and steadfast believer is part of the Party of Allah. «لاتجد قوماً یؤمنون... یوادّون من حادّ اللّه... اولئك حزب اللّه»

  12. Victory and dominance are valuable only when accompanied by salvation and the achievement of the goal. Elsewhere He says: «ألا انّ حزب اللّه هم الغالبون» (62), and here He says: «ألا انّ حزب اللّه هم المفلحون»

  13. The continuation of blessing is itself one of the blessings of Paradise. «خالدین فیها»

  14. Our being satisfied with Allah is one side of the issue; what is important is Allah's satisfaction with us. «رضى اللّه عنهم و رضوا عنه»

  15. In the Hereafter, blessings are of two types: material «جنات...» and spiritual. «رضى اللّه عنهم...» (As He says elsewhere: «و رضوان من اللّه اكبر» (63)).
